goal: Sub 2:30 · Kiprun Singapore Half Marathon · 27 Sep 2026

run_structure.saturday: Key run (long run or tempo, alternating)

training_principles.polarized_80_20: With 2 runs/week: Wednesday is ALWAYS easy (HR < easy_max). Saturday alternates between long run and tempo.
training_principles.10_percent_rule: Total weekly km must not increase by more than 10% week-over-week. Flag if exceeded.
training_principles.cutback_weeks: Every 3rd week is a cutback: reduce long run by 30% and easy run by 20%. Essential for adaptation.
training_principles.long_run_cap: Long run should never exceed 55-60% of total weekly volume.
training_principles.recovery: 48-72 hours between runs with only 2x/week is built in. If RHR is elevated >5 bpm above baseline, suggest extra rest.
training_principles.heat_adjustment: Singapore heat adds 10-15 bpm to HR. Pace should be guided by HR, not speed. If HR exceeds zone, slow down — never push through.
training_principles.rpe_guidance: Easy runs: RPE 3-4 (can hold a conversation). Tempo: RPE 6-7 (can speak in short sentences). Long runs: RPE 4-5 (comfortable but purposeful).

hr_zones.notes: Singapore heat adds 10-15 bpm vs cool conditions. All HR targets already adjusted for heat. Guide by HR, not pace — pace will vary day to day.

training_phases.name: Base Building
training_phases.focus: Build aerobic base and running habit. All runs easy (HR < 150). No tempo work yet.

training_phases.name: Build Phase 1
training_phases.focus: Introduce tempo segments. Long run extends. 80/20 polarization begins.
training_phases.saturday: Wk1: Long 12 km (easy). Wk2: Tempo 8 km (2 km warm-up, 4 km @ 7:00-7:15/km, 2 km cool-down).

training_phases.name: Pre-Travel Taper
training_phases.focus: Reduce volume by 40%. Maintain intensity on one short tempo. Stay fresh for Norway.

training_phases.name: Norway Hiking
training_phases.focus: Active cross-training. ~79 km hiking, 7,400 m elevation. Maintain aerobic fitness through hiking. No running pressure.

training_phases.name: Return to Running
training_phases.focus: Ease back in after hiking. Very easy effort, short distances. Legs need 5-7 days to readapt to road impact.
training_phases.wednesday: Easy 4-5 km (very gentle, walk breaks OK)

training_phases.name: Peak Block
training_phases.focus: Race-specific training. Long runs reach 18 km. Tempo at race pace (7:06/km). This is the hardest 3 weeks.
training_phases.wednesday: Easy 6 km OR tempo 6 km (alternate weeks)
training_phases.saturday_progression: Wk1: Long 14 km. Wk2: Long 16 km. Wk3 (cutback): Long 12 km.

training_phases.name: Race Taper
training_phases.focus: Reduce volume 50%. Keep 1-2 short race-pace strides. Rest legs, trust the training. Carb-load Thu-Sat.

training_phases.name: RACE DAY
training_phases.focus: Sub 2:30. Start conservative (7:15-7:20/km first 5 km), settle into 7:00-7:06/km, negative split if possible. Hydrate every 3 km.
